name: Dave Barbour
abstract: David Michael Barbour (May 28, 1912 – December 11, 1965) was an American jazz guitarist. He was married to singer Peggy Lee and was her co-writer, accompanist, and bandleader.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: Dave Barbour was at Theta Del, he was the pianist... And he was the only one in the band that could read music
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing with Dave Barbour on guitar and Jimmie Rowles on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: After that Red started this small group with Dave Barbour and Stuie Pletcher and Herbie Haymer, Pete Peterson. ... [INTERVIEWER] ... Donald McCork, Herbie Haymer, you, Dave Barbour ... and Red. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: We had Dave Barbour on guitar... Dave Barbour and I and Pete Peterson

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And when he first came to the west coast in the early forties in Axel Stordahl's band, there was Dave Barbour, Herbie Haymer, Ziggy Elman,...

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I used to know the original editor of it -- Dave -- what's Dave's last name? He was connected with Capitol Records, Hollywood
